# lex-os CLI reference (generated from `--help`; do not edit by hand)
# Regenerate: UPDATE_CLI_REF=1 cargo test -p lex-os --test cli_contract

===== lex-os =====
Autonomous-agent runtime: a sealed box plus a goal, supervised from outside.

Usage: lex-os [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  run         Dispatch an agent against a manifest and run the mediation loop to a terminal outcome
  resolve     Negotiate a manifest against the (simulated) host and show what it resolves to — without running anything
  manifest    Manifest utilities
  audit       Audit-log utilities
  check       Type-check an agent Lex program against a manifest grant and refuse it if its effects exceed the grant — the type-check wall (demo Attempt 1), run *before* the program executes
  introspect  Emit the acli command tree for agent discovery
  box-smoke   Standalone Wall-2 proof: boot a real Firecracker microVM with the host-side egress wall, dwell while the guest boots and runs `/sbin/init.demo` (the egress probes), then tear it down. Streams the guest console. Requires `--features firecracker`, a KVM host, and root
  help        Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --output <OUTPUT>  Output format (text for humans, json for agents) [default: text] [possible values: text, json]
  -h, --help             Print help
  -V, --version          Print version

===== lex-os run =====
Dispatch an agent against a manifest and run the mediation loop to a terminal outcome

Usage: lex-os run [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --manifest <MANIFEST>
          Path to a manifest JSON file. Omit to use the built-in demo

      --output <OUTPUT>
          Output format (text for humans, json for agents)
          
          [default: text]
          [possible values: text, json]

      --audit-out <AUDIT_OUT>
          Write the resulting tamper-proof audit log here

      --namespaces-only
          Pretend the host can only do namespace isolation

      --offline
          Pretend the host has no outbound network

      --agent <AGENT>
          LLM backend to use as the agent brain (default: demo)

          Possible values:
          - demo:      Scripted deterministic demo agent (no model required)
          - ollama:    Local Ollama model (default: mistral). No egress needed
          - anthropic: Anthropic Claude (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var required)
          - open-ai:   OpenAI-compatible endpoint (OPENAI_API_KEY env var required)
          - guest:     Spawn lex-os-guest as a subprocess (stdio transport). The guest binary calls Ollama itself. Use this on macOS for a full end-to-end test without Firecracker; on Linux with --features firecracker the guest runs inside the microVM over vsock instead
          - robot:     In-guest robot agent that issues move_to/grasp/run_policy as supervisor-mediated skills against the gym sidecar
          
          [default: demo]

      --model <MODEL>
          Model name for the selected backend (e.g. "mistral", "qwen2.5-coder")

      --ollama-url <OLLAMA_URL>
          Ollama base URL (default: http://localhost:11434)

      --guest-bin <GUEST_BIN>
          Path to lex-os-guest binary for --agent guest (default: next to this exe)

      --guest-script <GUEST_SCRIPT>
          Deterministic in-guest script for --agent guest (e.g. "reprovision-demo"), in place of the LLM loop. Used by demos that need a fixed sequence

      --simulated
          Use the in-process simulated perimeter instead of a real microVM. NOT a security boundary — for portability/tests/dev. Also via LEX_OS_SIMULATED=1. Without it, a non-KVM host refuses rather than silently downgrading

      --jail-uid <JAIL_UID>
          uid firecracker is dropped to under the jailer (e.g. the invoking user)

      --jail-gid <JAIL_GID>
          gid firecracker runs as — use the `kvm` group so /dev/kvm is reachable

      --jail-base <JAIL_BASE>
          Base directory for the per-VM chroot
          
          [default: /srv/jailer]

      --jail-id <JAIL_ID>
          Per-VM jail id (chroot + cgroup name); kept stable across reprovisions
          
          [default: lex-os]

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

===== lex-os resolve =====
Negotiate a manifest against the (simulated) host and show what it resolves to — without running anything

Usage: lex-os resolve [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --manifest <MANIFEST>  
      --output <OUTPUT>      Output format (text for humans, json for agents) [default: text] [possible values: text, json]
      --namespaces-only      
      --offline              
  -h, --help                 Print help

===== lex-os manifest =====
Manifest utilities

Usage: lex-os manifest [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  sample  Print the built-in demo manifest as JSON
  hash    Print the content-address (hash) of a manifest
  narrow  Validate that a child manifest only *narrows* a parent (grant, egress allowlist, and budgets). Rejects any widening — the narrowing-invariant wall (demo Attempt 3)
  help    Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --output <OUTPUT>  Output format (text for humans, json for agents) [default: text] [possible values: text, json]
  -h, --help             Print help

===== lex-os manifest sample =====
Print the built-in demo manifest as JSON

Usage: lex-os manifest sample [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --output <OUTPUT>  Output format (text for humans, json for agents) [default: text] [possible values: text, json]
  -h, --help             Print help

===== lex-os manifest hash =====
Print the content-address (hash) of a manifest

Usage: lex-os manifest hash [OPTIONS] --manifest <MANIFEST>

Options:
      --manifest <MANIFEST>  
      --output <OUTPUT>      Output format (text for humans, json for agents) [default: text] [possible values: text, json]
  -h, --help                 Print help

===== lex-os manifest narrow =====
Validate that a child manifest only *narrows* a parent (grant, egress allowlist, and budgets). Rejects any widening — the narrowing-invariant wall (demo Attempt 3)

Usage: lex-os manifest narrow [OPTIONS] --parent <PARENT> --child <CHILD>

Options:
      --output <OUTPUT>  Output format (text for humans, json for agents) [default: text] [possible values: text, json]
      --parent <PARENT>  
      --child <CHILD>    
  -h, --help             Print help

===== lex-os audit =====
Audit-log utilities

Usage: lex-os audit [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  verify  Verify the hash chain of an audit log file
  render  Render an audit log as newline-delimited JSON (one entry per line) for a live, tailable external view
  tail    Follow an audit log file, printing new entries as they arrive (one NDJSON line per entry). Runs indefinitely; press Ctrl-C to stop
  help    Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --output <OUTPUT>  Output format (text for humans, json for agents) [default: text] [possible values: text, json]
  -h, --help             Print help

===== lex-os audit verify =====
Verify the hash chain of an audit log file

Usage: lex-os audit verify [OPTIONS] --log <LOG>

Options:
      --log <LOG>        
      --output <OUTPUT>  Output format (text for humans, json for agents) [default: text] [possible values: text, json]
  -h, --help             Print help

===== lex-os audit render =====
Render an audit log as newline-delimited JSON (one entry per line) for a live, tailable external view

Usage: lex-os audit render [OPTIONS] --log <LOG>

Options:
      --log <LOG>        
      --output <OUTPUT>  Output format (text for humans, json for agents) [default: text] [possible values: text, json]
  -h, --help             Print help

===== lex-os audit tail =====
Follow an audit log file, printing new entries as they arrive (one NDJSON line per entry). Runs indefinitely; press Ctrl-C to stop

Usage: lex-os audit tail [OPTIONS] --log <LOG>

Options:
      --log <LOG>        
      --output <OUTPUT>  Output format (text for humans, json for agents) [default: text] [possible values: text, json]
  -h, --help             Print help

===== lex-os check =====
Type-check an agent Lex program against a manifest grant and refuse it if its effects exceed the grant — the type-check wall (demo Attempt 1), run *before* the program executes

Usage: lex-os check [OPTIONS] --grant <GRANT> <PROGRAM>

Arguments:
  <PROGRAM>  The agent `.lex` program to check

Options:
      --grant <GRANT>    Manifest JSON whose grant + egress the program is checked against
      --output <OUTPUT>  Output format (text for humans, json for agents) [default: text] [possible values: text, json]
  -h, --help             Print help

===== lex-os introspect =====
Emit the acli command tree for agent discovery

Usage: lex-os introspect [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --output <OUTPUT>  Output format (text for humans, json for agents) [default: text] [possible values: text, json]
  -h, --help             Print help
